stem: Let \( \mathbf{F}(x,y,z) = \left( ([[a]])x + ([[p]])y,\; ([[q]])x + ([[b]])y,\; ([[c]])z \right) \). Find the \( \mathbf{k} \)-component (the coefficient of \( \mathbf{k} \)) of the curl \( \nabla \times \mathbf{F} \).
solution: The \( \mathbf{k} \)-component of the curl is \( \partial_x Q - \partial_y P \). Here \( \partial_x Q = [[q]] \) and \( \partial_y P = [[p]] \), so the component is \( ([[q]]) - ([[p]]) = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This reverses the subtraction, computing \( \partial_y P - \partial_x Q \) instead of \( \partial_x Q - \partial_y P \).
This adds the two partials instead of subtracting them.
This multiplies the two partials instead of subtracting them.
This is off by one from the correct component.
This is off by one from the correct component.
This is off by two from the correct component.
This is off by two from the correct component.

stem: Let \( \mathbf{F}(x,y,z) = \left( ([[a]])y^{2},\; ([[b]])x^{2},\; 0 \right) \). Find the \( \mathbf{k} \)-component of the curl \( \nabla \times \mathbf{F} \) at the point \( ([[x0]], [[y0]], 0) \).
solution: With \( P=([[a]])y^{2} \) and \( Q=([[b]])x^{2} \), the \( \mathbf{k} \)-component of the curl is \( \partial_x Q - \partial_y P = 2([[b]])x - 2([[a]])y \), which is not constant. Evaluating at \( ([[x0]], [[y0]], 0) \) gives \( 2([[b]])([[x0]]) - 2([[a]])([[y0]]) = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This reverses the subtraction order, computing \( \partial_y P - \partial_x Q \) instead.
This mismatches each partial with the wrong coordinate.
This forgets the factor of 2 that comes from differentiating each squared term.
This has the sign of the curl component reversed.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by two from the correct value.

stem: Let \( \mathbf{F}(x,y,z) = \left( ([[a]])y,\; kx,\; ([[c]])z \right) \), where \( k \) is a constant. For what value of \( k \) is \( \mathbf{F} \) irrotational (\( \nabla \times \mathbf{F} = \mathbf{0} \) everywhere)?
solution: The \( \mathbf{k} \)-component of the curl is \( \partial_x(kx) - \partial_y(([[a]])y) = k - ([[a]]) \); the other two components are automatically 0 for this field. Setting \( k - ([[a]]) = 0 \) gives \( k = ([[a]]) = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This negates the required value instead of matching it directly.
This is the unrelated \( z \)-coefficient \( c \), which never affects the curl.
This is the negative of the unrelated \( z \)-coefficient \( c \).
This doubles the correct coefficient instead of matching it.
This is off by one from the correct value of \( k \).
This is off by one from the correct value of \( k \).
This is off by two from the correct value of \( k \).
